cap_notes: Lamar Jackson restructure freed approximately $40M in cap space, creating more room post-FA than pre-FA on paper. OTC tracks $54.95M remaining as of 3/13.

key_departures.contract_signed: 3yr/$81M ($60M guaranteed, $27M AAV — record for interior OL)
key_departures.impact: Three-time Pro Bowl center and one of the top 5 centers in the NFL. Left for a record-setting deal the Ravens could not match under cap constraints. Leaves a massive void at C with only UDFA Corey Bullock as an in-house option.
Followed new NYG head coach John Harbaugh. Was the secondary receiving TE behind Mark Andrews. Ravens now thin at TE depth and pass-catching at the position behind Andrews.

key_departures.contract_signed: 3yr/$24.3M — highest-paid blocking TE in NFL history
key_departures.impact: Primary blocking TE and key run-game piece for Derrick Henry. Combined with Likely's departure, Ravens lost both non-Andrews TEs. Replaced by Durham Smythe but at a significant talent downgrade.
Starting deep safety who paired well with Kyle Hamilton and Malaki Starks. Departure leaves a meaningful hole at S3/deep safety. Addressed by Hawkins signing.
Coming off Achilles tear in OTAs, played only 4 games in 2025. Depth loss but limited impact given injury history. Part of the mass departure to Harbaugh's Giants.

Deadline acquisition who provided 2.5 sacks and 32 pressures in 9 games. Departure partially mitigated by Hendrickson signing — Jones would have been a rotational piece behind the starter. Team still needs depth EDGE opposite Hendrickson.
Veteran depth EDGE who produced career-high 12.5 sacks in 2024 but fell to just 2 in 2025. Was considering return on minimum deal. Marginal impact given Hendrickson signing and Mike Green returning.
All-Pro, Pro Bowl punter in his contract year who led the NFL in net punting average. Major special teams loss; Ravens must find a replacement.

Six-time Pro Bowl fullback who was a critical blocker for Derrick Henry's ground game. His presence on field correlated with Henry's most explosive runs. Loss hurts the run game; new OC Doyle reportedly leans 12 personnel so FB usage may be reduced anyway.
Starting RG who ranked 32nd in pass block win rate. Replaced by John Simpson; not a significant football loss.
Voiding contract at start of league year. Provided depth and contested-catch ability but was underutilized. WR room remains Flowers/Bateman led. Hopkins could potentially return on cheap deal.
Special teams ace who led Ravens in ST snaps. Depth/ST loss only.
Not tendered as RFA. Ravens have Derrick Henry, Justice Hill, and Rasheen Ali. Depth only departure.

signings.analysis: Four-time Pro Bowler and 2024 NFL sacks leader (17.5). Since 2020, his 74.5 sacks rank third in the NFL. The $28M AAV is elite-tier at EDGE and the $60M guaranteed reflects genuine long-term investment. Note: missed 10 games in 2025 with hip/pelvis surgery — health is the key question. But on talent and contract structure, EDGE goes from a Tier 1 critical need to a managed need. Ravens still retain Mike Green and Tavius Robinson. Some developmental EDGE depth still useful in the draft.

Returning reunion signing — Simpson started all 17 games at LG for Baltimore in 2023 before 2 strong seasons with the Jets. The 3yr/$30M is a legitimate starter investment at guard. He addresses one of the two guard spots. However, CENTER remains a critical unaddressed need — Linderbaum's departure to LV for $27M AAV was not replaced. Corey Bullock (UDFA backup) is the only in-house option. IOL need is split: guard is partially addressed, C is a critical outstanding hole. Overall IOL: partially addressed. Note: discipline concerns (led NFL in penalty yards in 2025).

2yr/$10M ($5M AAV) is below the starter-tier threshold for S. However, Hawkins is expected to be a genuine starter as the S3 alongside Hamilton and Starks. PFF graded him No. 5 overall safety in 2025 (83.3 grade), 4 INTs, 15 starts for AFC champion Patriots. This is a below-market contract for above-average production — a Ravens specialty signing. The S need caused by losing both Gilman and Washington is partially addressed: Hawkins replaces Gilman's role meaningfully but the lack of guarantees reported and 2-year structure signals more of a bridge than a franchise-level solve. Malaki Starks is the long-term answer. S drops from critical to moderate need.

Extension signed in December 2025, locking up the franchise's all-time leader in receptions (473), receiving yards (5,862), and TDs (56). The 3yr/$39.3M with $26M guaranteed is a real starter-level commitment at TE. Andrews as the TE1 is secured. However, the departures of BOTH Isaiah Likely AND Charlie Kolar leave the TE room critically thin behind him. Durham Smythe was signed for the blocking role but is a clear step-down from Kolar. A pass-catching TE2 is still a priority need. TE: partially addressed — anchor secured, depth is the remaining hole.

Brought in as the Charlie Kolar blocking replacement. Smythe blocked on 74.1% of his snaps in Chicago last year and is familiar with new OC Declan Doyle's system. However, PFF graded him as its LOWEST-graded TE in 2025. Terms not fully disclosed but likely a modest deal. This is a warm-body/system-fit signing for the blocking TE role — it does not address the pass-catching TE2 void. TE depth minimally addressed; pass-catching TE2 still a clear need.

Re-signing of proven backup who led Ravens to two wins in relief of Lamar Jackson in 2025. QB was never a need with Jackson. This secures an important safety valve role given Jackson's injury history. Cooper Rush released as a result. Minimal need impact.

One-year, $5M fully guaranteed deal to retain last year's best value signing. Awuzie is a solid CB3 behind Wiggins and Humphrey. PFF graded him 13th among CBs (74.7) with 27.6% forced tight-window throw rate. However, one-year deals signal bridge/depth — this does NOT address CB depth long-term. Durability concerns (missed 34 games in 6 seasons). CB3 is maintained; long-term CB depth is still a draft concern.

One-year depth signing of a C/G versatile backup who played only 11 offensive snaps in 2025 with Atlanta. Reunites with new OL coach Dwayne Ledford. PFF grade of 81.2 is solid but came almost entirely on special teams. The value here is scheme familiarity and positional versatility (can play C or G), potentially audition for a starting center role. Does not address the Linderbaum void meaningfully — more of a developmental camp body who could surprise.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Hendrickson signing (4yr/$112M, $60M guaranteed, $28M AAV) is an elite-tier investment that fills the Tier 1 pass-rush void. Four-time Pro Bowler, 2024 sacks leader. Mike Green and Tavius Robinson also return. Some developmental depth still useful in Day 2-3 but no longer a critical need. Caveat: Hendrickson missed 10 games in 2025 with surgery — if health doesn't hold, this could escalate back. For now: drop to Tier 3.

Linderbaum's departure to LV on a record $27M AAV deal CREATES a new Tier 1 center need. Simpson's 3yr/$30M partially addresses guard, but center remains completely unresolved. Corey Bullock is the only in-house C option and is an unproven UDFA. Gwyn is a camp-level depth signing. IOL overall escalates from Tier 2 to Tier 1, driven by the unaddressed center hole. This is arguably the most pressing remaining need heading into the draft.

Mark Andrews (3yr/$39.3M) secures the TE1 position. However, losing BOTH Isaiah Likely (3yr/$40M, Giants) AND Charlie Kolar (3yr/$24.3M, Chargers) in the same week leaves the TE room dangerously thin. Durham Smythe is a minimum-deal blocking placeholder and PFF's lowest-graded TE in 2025. A pass-catching TE2 is a real need. Tier escalates from 3 to 2 — should address in Rounds 2-4 of the draft.

Losing both Gilman (3yr/$24.75M) and Ar'Darius Washington hurts, but the Hawkins signing (2yr/$10M, PFF's No. 5 overall S in 2025) fills the S3/deep safety role admirably at value pricing. Hamilton and Starks are the foundation. S drops from Tier 2 to Tier 3 — depth signing adequate for now, but no long-term anchor beyond Hawkins's 2-year bridge.

Awuzie re-signed on 1yr/$5M maintains the CB3 spot. Wiggins and Humphrey are intact as CB1/CB2. Durability concerns persist with Awuzie and Humphrey approaching 30. CB stays at Tier 3 — depth/succession planning worth addressing in the middle rounds.

No moves made at IDL. Nnamdi Madubuike's status after a neck injury remains uncertain — if he can't play, this escalates to Tier 1. Taven Bryan and Brent Urban both departed (depth pieces). Travis Jones is the one reliable piece. IDL remains Tier 2 and may need a draft solution at DT.

DeAndre Hopkins voided off the roster; no replacement signed at WR as of 3/13. Flowers and Bateman lead the room. Bateman had a career-low 224 yards in 2025 and is a trade rumor candidate. WR depth is thin but Flowers is elite and the team runs heavy. Stays at Tier 3 — a developmental or value WR in the mid-rounds makes sense.

NEW NEED. Jordan Stout's departure to the Giants (3yr/$12.3M, record punter deal) leaves a significant special teams hole. Stout was All-Pro and Pro Bowl in his contract year, averaging a career-high 44.9 net yards per punt. No replacement signed as of 3/13. Punter is now a legitimate need — could be addressed via mid-round draft pick or later free agency signing.

key_additions.headline: Elite 4x Pro Bowl pass rusher (2024 sacks leader, 17.5 sacks) fills the Ravens' biggest defensive need after Crosby trade collapse
Reunion signing upgrades guard room after Linderbaum departure; started all 17 games at LG for BAL in 2023
PFF's No. 5-graded safety in 2025 (4 INTs, 15 starts) replaces both Gilman and Washington departures at S3
Franchise all-time leader in receptions, yards, and TDs locked up through 2028 as TE1 anchor
Blocking TE replacement for Charlie Kolar; reunites with new Ravens OC Declan Doyle from Chicago
Proven dual-threat backup re-signed after going 2-0 in relief of Jackson in 2025; Cooper Rush released
Best-value CB3 in the league returns on one-year deal after ranking 13th in PFF CB grades in 2025
Versatile C/G depth signing reunites with OL coach Ledford; camp competition for center role

key_losses.contract_summary: 3yr/$81M ($60M gtd) with Raiders — record for interior OL
key_losses.headline: 3x Pro Bowl center departs for record-breaking contract; leaves massive unresolved hole at C
Pass-catching TE2 follows coach Harbaugh to NYG; Ravens lose both non-Andrews TEs in same week

key_losses.contract_summary: 3yr/$24.3M with Chargers — record for blocking TE
key_losses.headline: Elite blocking TE and critical Henry run-game piece departs; biggest scheme loss in free agency
Starting deep safety joins AFC rival; Hawkins signing mitigates but does not fully replace
Productive mid-season trade acquisition departs; Hendrickson signing largely negates impact
All-Pro, Pro Bowl punter (44.9 net avg) follows Harbaugh to NYG; creates a new unaddressed need
6x Pro Bowl fullback and critical Derrick Henry run-game blocker follows Harbaugh; run game impact TBD
